title: Selectable Watches
text: Selectable Watches Select fields, variables, and ProcScript functions to add to the Watches pane. The list of Selectable Watches is determined by the Scope and Function fields. When the form starts, it can be refreshed with the Refresh command button, but only if the debugger is in control. Watches are set for all of the selected items, unless they exist in the Watches pane of the debugger. Refer to these topics: Selectable Watches list Selectable Watches buttons Selectable Watches fields Editing To understand how to edit Selectable Watches, refer to Working with watches . Scope Specify the scope of items available to watch—application, component, entity, or field. The Functions list displays items appropriate to the Scope . For example, if you choose Application , Functions displays application-level functions. If you choose Component , Functions displays component variables and component-level functions. Functions Specify the categories (ProcScript functions, fields, or variables) applicable to the selected Scope . The category that you specify determines the contents of the list of Selectable Watches . For example, if you select Entity in Scope , and select Field values (all painted entities) in Functions , a list of field names (and the entity names in which these fields occur) is displayed in the Selectable Watches . Related Links Watches Watches are designated fields, variables, expressions, and functions that are continuously monitored while the application or component is debugged. Was this topic helpful?
